{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Slings are bearing over unprotected sharp edges without edge protection, creating a cutting/shearing hazard that can sever or damage the sling under load",
    "Rigging configuration does not meet ASME B30 requirements for protecting slings from damage at contact points",
    "OSHA 1926.251 requires slings be protected from sharp corners and edges; current configuration is non-compliant"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop the lift immediately and lower or secure the load",
    "Install suitable edge protection (riggers pads, hardwood dunnage, or engineered protectors) at all sling-to-edge contact points",
    "Inspect slings for damage from contact with unprotected edges before returning to service",
    "Re-inspect rigging configuration after edge protection is installed before proceeding with the lift"
  ],
  "rationale": "Slings over unprotected edges violate both ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251, which require that slings be protected from cutting or damage at all contact points with sharp edges. This is a critical safety hazard that can cause sling failure and dropped load. The work is rough-complete but cannot proceed to closure without correction.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30 (rigging safety standard — sling protection requirements)",
    "OSHA 1926.251 (rigging equipment for material handling — sling use and protection)"
  ]
}
